edges.description: TELO2/TTT-complex dysfunction perturbs neurodevelopment, producing global developmental delay.
TELO2/TTT-complex dysfunction is linked to the defining intellectual disability phenotype.
Broader neurodevelopmental disruption in You-Hoover-Fong syndrome can include microcephaly.
Developmental disruption in TELO2-related disease can include congenital lens opacity.
Multisystem developmental involvement in TELO2-related disease can include hearing impairment.
Multisystem developmental involvement in TELO2-related disease can include congenital cardiac malformation.

nodes.description: Lens opacity present at or soon after birth.

evidence.reference_title: Novel compound heterozygous mutations in TELO2 in an infant with You-Hoover-Fong syndrome: A case report and literature review.

nodes.description: Congenital cardiac malformations including atrial septal defect.

evidence.reference_title: Novel compound heterozygous mutations in TELO2 in an infant with You-Hoover-Fong syndrome: A case report and literature review.

nodes.description: Severely delayed motor and cognitive milestones from infancy.

evidence.reference_title: Novel compound heterozygous mutations in TELO2 in an infant with You-Hoover-Fong syndrome: A case report and literature review.
evidence.snippet: global development delay complicated with postnatal growth retardation for more than 1 year

evidence.reference_title: Novel compound heterozygous mutations in TELO2 in an infant with You-Hoover-Fong syndrome: A case report and literature review.
Novel compound heterozygous mutations in TELO2 in an infant with You-Hoover-Fong syndrome: A case report and literature review.
CLK-2/TEL2 is a conserved component of the nonsense-mediated mRNA decay pathway.
evidence.snippet: variants of TELO2 have been linked to an intellectual disability disorder, the You-Hoover-Fong syndrome

evidence.reference_title: Novel compound heterozygous mutations in TELO2 in an infant with You-Hoover-Fong syndrome: A case report and literature review.

nodes.description: Mutations in TELO2 destabilize the TTT co-chaperone complex (TELO2-TTI1-TTI2), which is essential for co-translational maturation and stability of PIKKs including ATM, ATR, DNA-PKcs, mTOR, SMG1, and TRRAP. Loss of TELO2 leads to reduced PIKK protein levels, compromising DNA damage checkpoints, growth signaling via mTOR, and nonsense-mediated mRNA decay via SMG1.

evidence.reference_title: CLK-2/TEL2 is a conserved component of the nonsense-mediated mRNA decay pathway.
evidence.snippet: the human counterpart of CLK-2/TEL2, TELO2, has been also implicated in the NMD, suggesting a conserved role of CLK-2/TEL2 proteins in mRNA surveillance
